features.summary: Tesla reported Q2 2022 results despite Shanghai factory shutdowns due to COVID lockdowns. The company achieved production records at Fremont and Shanghai in June, positioning for a strong second half. Automotive gross margin declined to 27.9% due to Shanghai production impacts, factory restart costs, and ramp inefficiencies at new Austin and Berlin facilities. Tesla sold most Bitcoin holdings for liquidity during China uncertainties but maintained Dogecoin. The company deployed FSD beta to over 100,000 owners with 35 million autonomous miles driven.
features.management_outlook: Management expects a record-breaking second half of 2022, targeting 50% annual growth despite increased difficulty. Cybertruck production is planned for mid-2023, with FSD beta rollout to all North American customers by year-end. Austin and Berlin factories are ramping toward 5,000 cars per week by year-end, with potential for 10,000 weekly by end of 2023. The company anticipates reaching 40,000 vehicle production per week by Q4 2022, utilizing installed capacity of 1.9 million annual units.
features.risks: Key risks include potential force majeure events, ongoing supply chain constraints, and semiconductor shortages in older generation chips. Inflation and commodity cost pressures continue impacting margins, though some commodities show declining trends. Austin and Berlin ramp inefficiencies will persist through 2022. COVID-related shutdowns remain a concern, particularly in China. Macroeconomic uncertainty could affect demand, though current backlog provides substantial buffer with delivery times extending into 2023.
features.key_drivers: Production ramp at new factories is the primary growth driver, with 4680 battery cell production targeting over 1,000 per week by year-end. Structural battery pack architecture represents fundamental competitive advantage despite current C-grade implementation. FSD technology advancement with 120-person AI team continues toward full autonomy. Pricing power remains strong due to overwhelming demand exceeding production capacity. Energy business achieved record gross profit with highest solar volumes in years.
